npcs.name: Marcus Chen
npcs.bio: A fearless starter who loves head-to-head showdowns.
npcs.dossier_bio: Marcus thrives on the tension of a close race and will trade leads all the way through the line. {He} spent the summer drilling block starts with {his} club coach, and the improvement shows -- {his} reaction times are among the best in the classification. Opponents who let {him} get to the 60-meter mark even will not like how the last 40 plays out.

npcs.name: Carlos Reyes
npcs.bio: Technically sharp hurdler with late-race composure.
npcs.dossier_bio: Carlos is a four-event qualifier who has placed at every level from class meets to nationals. {His} three-step pattern between hurdles is textbook, and {he} rarely loses form even when fatigued in the 400mH. Coaches from across the state point to {him} as the standard for technical hurdling at the prep level.

npcs.name: Tyler Patton
npcs.bio: Grinder who wins by refusing to fade.
npcs.dossier_bio: Tyler is the kind of runner who makes you earn every place. {He} runs high mileage in the offseason and it pays off in the second half of every distance race. Not the fastest guy on any start list, but {he} is relentless -- {he} has a habit of picking off fading runners over the final 800 meters.

npcs.name: Darius Whitfield
npcs.bio: Top-ten nationally. You know {his} name before the meet starts.
npcs.dossier_bio: Darius is the headliner. {He} has run sub-10.5 in the 100m and can drop a 47-flat 400m when the field pushes {him}. College scouts have been tracking {him} since sophomore year, and {he} carries himself like someone who already knows where the next level is. When {he} walks into a meet, the seedings shift.

npcs.name: Elias Solis
npcs.bio: Patient kicker -- {he} runs the first three laps to set up the last 200.
npcs.dossier_bio: Elias has the rare ability to look relaxed at a pace that would bury most runners. {He} sits in the pack through 1200 meters and then unleashes a finishing kick that has won {him} two state-level medals already. {His} coach calls {him} the most tactically mature runner on the team.

npcs.name: Kenji Okoro
npcs.bio: Built for the ring. {His} technique was cleaned up over the offseason.
npcs.dossier_bio: Kenji spent the offseason working with a private throws coach and the results are obvious -- {his} shot put spin is tighter and {his} discus release angle has flattened out. {He} competes in all three throws and scores in at least two at every meet. The raw power was always there; now the technique is catching up.

npcs.name: Priyan Nair
npcs.bio: {His} approach speed is elite. The technique showed up senior year.
npcs.dossier_bio: Priyan was always the fastest guy on the runway, but early in {his} career the jumps were inconsistent. That changed this year. {His} takeoff timing has locked in, and now {he} is converting that elite speed into marks that place at the regional level in all three jumping events. A serious multi-event threat when {he} is on.

npcs.name: Jaylen Cross
npcs.bio: Competing up every chance {he} gets. Strong finisher on both events.
npcs.dossier_bio: Jaylen is the kind of competitor who enters every invitational {he} can find, always looking for a faster field. {He} doubles the 400m and 800m with a quick turnaround and still runs hard in both. {His} finishing speed over the last 100 meters is better than {his} seed times suggest, and {he} is improving fast.

npcs.name: Oliver Shaw
npcs.bio: Ran a 9:02 as a junior. {He}'s been chasing the school record ever since.
npcs.dossier_bio: Oliver is a nationally ranked 3200m runner who trains like a college athlete already. {He} logs serious mileage year-round, and {his} race IQ is well beyond {his} age -- {he} knows exactly when to push the pace and when to sit. The school record is 8:54, and everyone in the program expects {him} to break it before the season ends.

npcs.name: Luca Bennett
npcs.bio: Quick out of the blocks and aggressive through the curve.
npcs.dossier_bio: Luca is a sophomore who already races like an upperclassman. {He} explodes out of the blocks with zero hesitation and attacks the 200m curve hard enough to unsettle runners in the lanes around {him}. The endurance is not quite there yet for longer sprints, but in anything under 25 seconds, {he} is a handful.

npcs.name: Malik Hayes
npcs.bio: Still rough between barriers, but fearless whenever the race gets messy.
npcs.dossier_bio: Malik clips hurdles, stutters between barriers, and still finishes close enough to scare you. {He} is a raw talent who converted from flat sprints last year and has not fully polished {his} technique. But {his} speed between the sticks is real, and in chaotic local-level races where everyone is rattled, that fearlessness is a weapon.

npcs.name: Devin Hart
npcs.bio: Clean trail leg, calm championship temperament, and almost no wasted motion.
npcs.dossier_bio: Devin is the most technically sound hurdler you will face at the national level. {His} trail leg clearance is textbook, {his} stride pattern between barriers never breaks down, and {he} races championship rounds like they are Tuesday practice reps. Multiple D1 programs are watching {him} closely, and the attention has not affected {his} focus one bit.

npcs.name: Nolan Price
npcs.bio: Controls the middle laps and dares everyone else to respond late.
npcs.dossier_bio: Nolan does not chase. {He} sets the pace from the gun, dares the field to follow, and then kicks again when everyone expects {him} to fade. {His} 800m-1600m double at last year's regional championship earned {him} two medals and a reputation as someone who races to break the field, not just to win.

npcs.name: Gavin Rowe
npcs.bio: Not flashy, but {he} keeps squeezing the pace until the field breaks.
npcs.dossier_bio: Gavin will never be the most talented runner in the race, and {he} does not care. {He} is methodical -- {he} trains consistently, runs within {himself}, and chips a second or two off {his} PRs every season. In the 3200m, that kind of steady pressure wears people down. By the final mile, most runners behind {him} have already given up.

npcs.name: Brady Sloan
npcs.bio: Strong enough to matter already, with just enough technique to steal local wins.
npcs.dossier_bio: Brady is a football lineman who throws shot put and discus in the spring because {his} football coach told {him} to. The surprising part is how much {he} actually likes it. {His} technique is still rough around the edges, but {he} is strong enough to win local meets on power alone. If {he} ever commits to the ring full-time, watch out.

npcs.name: Teo Morales
npcs.bio: A polished release point makes {his} best throws look effortless.
npcs.dossier_bio: Teo is the thrower other throwers study. {His} discus release is clean enough to make senior coaches stop and stare, and {his} javelin approach has a smoothness that belies how hard {he} is actually throwing. {He} has been a national qualifier in both events and carries the quiet confidence of someone who has already proven {himself} at the highest level.

npcs.name: Camden Ross
npcs.bio: Still learning the details, but {his} bounce shows up in every meet.
npcs.dossier_bio: Camden is all raw potential right now. {He} cleared 5-10 in high jump on pure athleticism before anyone taught {him} a proper Fosbury approach, and {his} long jump marks are creeping up as {his} runway mechanics improve. The ceiling is high if {he} sticks with it -- right now {he} is just the local kid who out-bounces everyone.

npcs.name: Zane Callow
npcs.bio: Big-meet jumper whose series usually gets better as the competition tightens.
npcs.dossier_bio: Zane is a championship-caliber multi-event jumper who somehow produces {his} best marks under the most pressure. In the long jump, {he} will foul the first two attempts and then rip a mark on the final jump that wins the competition. {He} competes across three jumping events at the national level and has medaled in two of them.

npcs.name: Deon Wiggins
npcs.bio: Raw athleticism that makes {him} unpredictable on the runway.
npcs.dossier_bio: Deon is a basketball player who jumped into track because {his} gym teacher saw {him} clear a park bench. {His} long jump approach is inconsistent and {his} triple jump phases are still out of rhythm, but the pure explosiveness is undeniable. On {his} best day, {he} can beat anyone at the local level -- the problem is nobody knows which day that will be.

npcs.name: Reggie Thorn
npcs.bio: All-out from the gun. Fades late but never lets you forget {he} was there.
npcs.dossier_bio: Reggie runs every race like it is {his} last. {He} takes the 100m out at full effort and attacks the 400m with the same reckless aggression, which means {he} is either winning by daylight at 200 meters or dying over the final straight. The inconsistency keeps {him} at the local level for now, but {he} is always a factor through the first half of the race.

npcs.name: Isaac Flores
npcs.bio: Patient front-runner who turns early leads into honest victories.
npcs.dossier_bio: Isaac is a smart, steady runner who likes to grab the lead early and control the pace from the front. {He} does not have a devastating kick, but {he} almost never blows up either -- {his} splits are remarkably even for a runner at the club level. The 800m and 1500m double is {his} bread and butter, and {he} handles the workload without complaint.

npcs.name: Hector Vega
npcs.bio: Heavy lifter who showed up to track on a dare and never left.
npcs.dossier_bio: Hector wandered over from the weight room on a bet and threw 38 feet on {his} first day. {His} technique is unrefined and {his} footwork in the ring is basically nonexistent, but the sheer strength makes {him} competitive at the local level in shot put and javelin. {He} genuinely enjoys the throwing events now and has started asking {his} coach for extra practice time.

npcs.name: Maya Thompson
npcs.bio: Explosive over the first half of every sprint and never shy about racing up a tier.
npcs.dossier_bio: Maya has one gear: full speed. {She} rockets out of the blocks and builds a gap before the field even settles into stride. The second half of {her} races is less polished -- {she} tends to tighten up late -- but at the local level, the damage is already done by then. {She} signs up for every invitational {she} can find, always looking for a faster heat.

npcs.name: Sienna Brooks
npcs.bio: Strong through the curve and dangerous whenever the 400 opens too fast.
npcs.dossier_bio: Sienna is a versatile sprinter who can line up in the 100m, 200m, or 400m and place in all three. {Her} real strength is the curve -- {she} runs relaxed through the bend while other runners tense up, and that efficiency gives {her} an edge over the back half. In the 400m, {she} feeds off fast openers and chases down runners who go out too hard.

npcs.name: Naomi Price
npcs.bio: National-class sprint talent with the kind of poise that changes a whole meet.
npcs.dossier_bio: Naomi is the most complete sprinter in the country at the prep level. {She} runs all three sprint distances at or near national-qualifying marks, and {she} does it with a calm that makes championship rounds feel routine. Coaches describe {her} as unflappable -- {she} has never visibly panicked in a race, even when the field is stacked. The college recruitment race for {her} is already well underway.

npcs.name: Alina Reyes
npcs.bio: Quick between hurdles and still getting sharper each month.
npcs.dossier_bio: Alina picked up hurdling as a sophomore and has been on a steep improvement curve ever since. {Her} speed between barriers is already class-level, and {her} technique gets noticeably better from meet to meet. {She} watches film of {her} own races and shows up the next week with adjustments. The ceiling is high if {she} keeps this trajectory.

npcs.name: Zoe Kim
npcs.bio: Technically polished hurdler who seems to get cleaner as the stakes rise.
npcs.dossier_bio: Zoe is a student of the event. {She} can tell you {her} three-step count to the inch, and {her} trail leg clearance barely changes from warmups to finals. What makes {her} dangerous at the state and regional level is that {she} actually gets sharper under pressure -- {her} best times have all come in championship heats, never in regular-season invitationals.

npcs.name: Danielle Okafor
npcs.bio: Every hurdle looks identical. That consistency is why {she} keeps winning major rounds.
npcs.dossier_bio: Danielle is the kind of hurdler who makes the event look mechanical -- every clearance at the same height, every landing in the same spot, every stride between barriers perfectly measured. That robotic consistency is what separates {her} from the field at nationals. {She} does not rely on one big race; {she} just never runs a bad one. Coaches call {her} the most reliable hurdler in the state.

npcs.name: Leila Hassan
npcs.bio: Settles into rhythm early and keeps making people pay for going out too hot.
npcs.dossier_bio: Leila is a one-event specialist who has turned the 800m into {her} personal laboratory. {She} studies splits, paces {her} training runs to the second, and refuses to get pulled into a fast first lap. At the club level, that patience is a weapon -- half the field goes out too hard, and Leila picks them off one by one in the final 300 meters.

npcs.name: Arianna Doyle
npcs.bio: She is never rattled by pace changes and always has a response over the final lap.
npcs.dossier_bio: Arianna is a competitor who seems to have an answer for every move. Surge at 600? {She} matches it. Slow the pace to a crawl? {She} sits and waits. {Her} 800m-1600m double has been a scoring machine at the state and regional level for two seasons, and {her} ability to read a race in real time gives {her} an edge that pure fitness alone cannot match.

npcs.name: Ella Morales
npcs.bio: Patient and punishing. She almost always saves the best move for the last 250.
npcs.dossier_bio: Ella is a predator in the back half of distance races. {She} sits behind the leaders with unsettling calm, waiting for the moment when the pace starts to bite. Then {she} moves, usually with 250 to go, and very few runners at the regional level can respond. {Her} 800m is {her} stronger event, but {she} doubles the 1600m just to keep {her} race sharpness up.

npcs.name: Hannah Sloan
npcs.bio: A steady grinder who keeps clipping the pace until the field gives in.
npcs.dossier_bio: Hannah is not flashy and does not pretend to be. {She} trains six days a week, runs consistent splits in every workout, and brings that same metronomic approach to race day. In the 1600m and 3200m, that consistency is devastating -- {she} does not blow anyone away, but {she} grinds down every runner who tries to hang with {her} over the final half-mile.

npcs.name: Brielle Nguyen
npcs.bio: National-level distance runner who controls races by making every lap honest.
npcs.dossier_bio: Brielle is the pace-setter nobody wants to follow. {She} runs from the front, keeps every lap within a second of the last, and dares the field to match {her} workload. At the national level, that strategy separates the contenders from the pretenders fast. {She} has qualified for nationals in both the 1600m and 3200m and is considered one of the top distance prospects in {her} class.

npcs.name: Madison Cole
npcs.bio: Looks calm no matter how punishing the pace gets, which is why {she} is still there at the bell.
npcs.dossier_bio: Madison is the runner you forget about until it is too late. {She} races at the back of the lead pack with a blank expression while the leaders burn matches, and then {she} floats past them over the final 400 like the first 1200 meters never happened. {Her} ability to absorb pain without showing it is legendary on the national circuit. Several elite D1 programs already have {her} on their radar.

npcs.name: Kennedy Wells
npcs.bio: Already strong enough to bully local fields when {her} release point cooperates.
npcs.dossier_bio: Kennedy showed up to the throws circle with a volleyball background and a frame that suggests {she} belongs there. {Her} shot put power is already enough to dominate local fields, and {her} discus has flashes of real distance when {her} release timing clicks. The technique is still a work in progress, but {she} is coachable and competitive, which means the ceiling is a long way off.

npcs.name: Samara Vega
npcs.bio: Multi-event thrower with enough technique to score in three places on the same day.
npcs.dossier_bio: Samara is the rare thrower who can score meaningful points in shot put, discus, and javelin at the same meet. {She} spent two years refining {her} technique across all three implements, and the consistency shows -- {she} rarely has a zero in any event. At the state level, that triple-scoring ability makes {her} one of the most valuable field athletes on any roster.

npcs.name: Iris Delgado
npcs.bio: The release is smooth, the runway is clean, and the marks keep stacking up.
npcs.dossier_bio: Iris is a technician. {Her} discus release has the kind of clean, repeatable motion that coaches build highlight reels around, and {her} javelin approach is just as polished. {She} does not have the brute strength of some throwers, but {her} consistency and timing produce marks that hold up at the regional level. When the competition matters most, {her} form never wavers.

npcs.name: Talia Rivera
npcs.bio: A natural jumper whose runway confidence keeps growing with every championship round.
npcs.dossier_bio: Talia has the kind of natural bounce that coaches notice immediately. {Her} long jump approach has gotten more aggressive each season, and {her} high jump technique -- which started as pure athleticism -- now includes a real Fosbury arc. {She} performs best at championship meets, where the adrenaline seems to add inches to {her} marks. A state-level contender in both events.

npcs.name: Nia Clarke
npcs.bio: Championship jumper whose best attempts usually arrive when the meet is on the line.
npcs.dossier_bio: Nia is the jumper everyone dreads seeing on the start list at a big meet. {She} competes in long jump, triple jump, and high jump at the national level, and {her} defining trait is that {she} saves {her} best for the final round. When the pressure is highest and one jump decides the medal, Nia almost always delivers. Multiple D1 programs are recruiting {her} as a multi-event prospect.

npcs.name: Jocelyn Park
npcs.bio: Consistent on the board and dangerous in meets where everyone else starts chasing fouls.
npcs.dossier_bio: Jocelyn wins meets by not beating herself. While other jumpers foul their way out of contention, {she} hits the board clean attempt after attempt and stacks solid marks. {Her} long jump and triple jump consistency has earned {her} placements from class meets all the way to regionals. {She} is not always the most explosive jumper in the field, but {she} is almost always the most reliable.
